conditions.message: The Spark ritual reduces shortcut risk by explicitly forbidding the low-tier gate operator from waiving or reinterpreting verifier results and by requiring hard stops on ambiguity, hash mismatch, dirty index, failed tests, and fail/unknown cells.
evidence: The staged text says Spark executes mechanics from prepared hash-bound handoffs, including index check, verifier begin, packet relay, cell wait, gate commit, attestation staging, second commit, push when required, bead close/sync, reservation release, and next wake.
The staged text says Spark never waives or reinterprets verifier results and never resolves ambiguity itself.
The hard-stop list includes hash mismatch, dirty index or peer dirt, verifier disagreement, any fail/unknown cell, test failure, and any state the handoff did not predict.
The purpose line frames Spark as cheap capacity for repeatable mechanics while expensive capacity stays on implementation and judgment cells, not as a way to bypass the fail-closed valve.
